REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANT MERCY HOUSING MANAGMENT GROUP, INC.'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. MOTION IS GRANTED AS TO THE REQUEST FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES UNDER CC 3294. NON-OPPOSITION FILED. MOTION IS OTHERWISE DENIED. SUFFICIENT FACTS ARE ALLEGED TO SUPPORT CLAIM FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES UNDER CC 1942.5 RE: CONSCIOUS DISREGARD OF PLAINTIFF'S RIGHTS AND SAFETY. REQUEST TO STRIKE REQUEST FOR EMOTIONAL DISTRESS DAMAGES IS DENIED. MOVING PARTY FAILED TO CITE ANY AUTHORITY FOR THE PROPOSITION THAT THESE TYPES OF DAMAGES ARE NEVER RECOVERABLE AS AN ITEM OF GENERAL OR SPECIAL DAMAGES UNDER THE ALLEGED CAUSES OF ACTION. DEFENDANT TO SUBMIT PROPOSED ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B). JUDGE RONALD EVANS QUIDACHAY, CLERK S. LE, NOT REPORTED (501)

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANT MERCY HOUSING MANAGMENT GROUP, INC. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED. MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED. MOVING PARTY FAILED TO COMPLY WITH CRC 3.1350(B). MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. ASSUMING THAT ALL EVIDENCE PROVIDED BY THE MOVING PARTY IS ADMISSIBLE, THERE STILL IS A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT REGARDING THE CONDITIONS, MAINTENANCE AND RESPONSE TIME TO TENANT'S CONCERNS. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Defendant Mercy Housing Management Group, Inc.S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment Or, In The Alternative Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Nov-02-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via CourtCall:
Gregory P. Brock, Esq, (510) 841-1171 for plaintiff Sienna Dunn.
Shirley C. Wang, 415-278-1422 for Defendant Mercy Housing Management Group.

Hearing is held. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT MERCY HOUSING MANAGMENT GROUP, INC. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative Summary Adjudication is DENIED. Motion for Summary Adjudication is DENIED. Moving party failed to comply with CRC 3.1350(b). Motion for Summary Judgment is DENIED. Assuming that all evidence provided by the moving party is admissible, there still is a triable issue of fact regarding the conditions, maintenance and response time to tenant's concerns. Prevailing party to submit order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b).

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-03-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Greg Brock, esq. (510-841-1171) for Plaintiff/opposing party Sienna Dunn, et al.
Davis Wang, esq. (415-278-1427) for Defendant/Moving party MERCY HOUSING MANAGMENT GROUP, INC..

DEFENDANT MERCY HOUSING MANAGMENT GROUP, INC.'s DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT and MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT are heard concurrently.

ARGUED: civil code 1942.5, fraud, oppression, malice, discrimination. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT MERCY HOUSING MANAGMENT GROUP, INC.'s MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Motion is granted as to the request for punitive damages under CC 3294. Non-opposition filed. Motion is otherwise denied. Sufficient facts are alleged to support claim for punitive damages under CC 1942.5 re: conscious disregard of Plaintiff's rights and safety. Request to strike request for emotional distress damages is denied. Moving party failed to cite any authority for the proposition that these types of damages are never recoverable as an item of general or special damages under the alleged causes of action. Defendant to submit proposed order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge Ronald Evans Quidachay, Clerk S. Le, not reported (501)

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-03-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Greg Brock, esq. (510-841-1171) for Plaintiff/opposing party Sienna Dunn, et al.
Davis Wang, esq. (415-278-1427) for Defendant/Moving party MERCY HOUSING MANAGMENT GROUP, INC..

DEFENDANT MERCY HOUSING MANAGMENT GROUP, INC.'s DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT and MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT are heard concurrently.

ARGUED: civil code 1942.5, fraud, oppression, malice, discrimination. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT MERCY HOUSING MANAGMENT GROUP, INC.'s DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is sustained in part and overruled in part. Demurrer is sustained without leave to amend at to SFRO Violation Causes of action. Non-opposition filed. Demurrer is otherwise overruled. Sufficient ultimate facts are alleged to state each cause of action. Defendant to submit proposed order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: S. Le, Not Reported. (D501)
